author: razorclients
content:
The following discussions were taken during the call

- Moving the fork into the header and out of the url
- Moving from engine/v2/ down to engine/v1/
- having both json and ssz co-existing and gradually making it compulsory past a certain fork per say 
- clients can start shipping this as fast as they want with a flag 
- after the fork beyond which it becomes compulosry json-engine rpc is retired
- /engine/vx/{fork}/payloads/witness would be the way to go for <https://github.com/ethereum/execution-apis/pull/773>
- progressive will not be used for now.when 7688 is sfi-ed,we can rework to make the design using progressives
- no specific devnet for this,can be added to the glamsterdam devnets and the flags can be enabled


unanswered questions/differed

- <https://github.com/ethereum/execution-apis/pull/793#issuecomment-4454204579>
- <https://github.com/ethereum/execution-apis/pull/793#discussion_r3435225563>


would request the client teams who havnt reviewed <https://github.com/ethereum/execution-apis/pull/793> to do so as it would be the single source of truth 🙂

author: barnabasbusa
content: yeah this is kinda tricky as new branches have the new flag old ones have the old. Would be good if you guys did a bit of an overlap where the experimental and non experimental flag is both working. cc <@504202741933932544>

author: etan_status
content: re SSZ engine, if you want a more proper one, <@449019668296892420> has some ideas on syncing additional branches (right now not possible), monitoring sync progress vs unavailable states (right now indistinguishable) etc, and then there's also the 7807 thing that makes block_hash == hash_tree_root so that CL can do validations autonomously and no logner needs the EL to optimistically sync (i.e., the nimbus behaviour where we just load the nimbus-EL library into the cl and do kzg and block hash validation locally if the EL is under maintenance). and then there's also the time of check / time of use bug which makes exchangeCapabilities kinda meh, because there may be EL upgrades/downgrades between requests, or an EL multiplexer may choose a different EL as one goes under maintenance.

author: tbenr
content: we don't normally care about breaking experimental flags (and you don't normally rush into adding them in kurtosis 🙂 ) this has been a pretty rare circumstance imo
